127.reason: [Before calling DFSTraverse after deletion, the deleted vertex 'de' must be marked as visited (or skipped), otherwise DFS may still try to traverse from or to it. But the graph structure was modified, yet 'visit[de]' is 0, so if somehow reached (which shouldn't happen), it would print. However, more critically, the traversal starts at 0 and the graph is connected without 'de', so it should work — but the real bug is in Delete() corrupting adjacency lists.]
